# Questions in Livestream 167

Live Stream #167: Super Expensive Egyptian Ritual Practice

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=434" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:14</a> | If Adam and Eve were special priestly creations rather than the first humans biologically, would that break the redemption narrative? | He says no and still treats Adam as the key covenantal figure through whom the problem of sin is framed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=667" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:07</a> | Does the Ipuwer Papyrus confirm the ten plagues? | He says no and treats the comparisons as loose analogies rather than direct confirmation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=979" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:19</a> | Did ancient Egyptian cities have parks? | He says no in the modern public-park sense and explains that Egyptian urban planning worked differently. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=1238" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:38</a> | What should people make of the Emerald Tablets of Thoth, reformed Egyptian, and similar claims? | He treats them as spurious modern religious claims rather than genuine ancient Egyptian traditions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=1616" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:56</a> | What material would he recommend for a father doing Bible study with children ages seven to thirteen? | He recommends a good children's Bible for younger children, a regular Bible for older children, and a parent-led study that lets the children ask questions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=1873" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:13</a> | Is the common picture of heaven as endless spouse-less work basically a capitalist caricature? | He says no and argues that work itself is not a curse and heaven should not be reduced to that caricature. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=2179" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">36:19</a> | What is the best way to learn ancient Hebrew without institutional training? | He says the best option is to take an introductory Hebrew course online, because self-teaching is very difficult. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=2280" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:00</a> | How many letters does Hebrew have? | He says he thinks it has about twenty-two letters, while noting that the vowel pointing makes it more complicated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=2484" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:24</a> | Was Eglon's palace at Jericho, as Bryant Wood claims? | He says the text points to Eglon ruling from Jericho, while the precise archaeology and location claims need careful handling. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=2913" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:33</a> | What commentaries does he recommend on the Pentateuch as a whole or on its individual books? | He gives resource guidance and emphasizes using serious book-specific commentaries rather than assuming one volume can do all the work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=3573" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:33</a> | Is he familiar with the criticisms Preston Sprinkle received from Rosaria Butterfield, and what are his thoughts? | He gives a limited answer, distinguishing what he knows from what he has not followed closely. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=3724" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:04</a> | What resources can help with ancient Near Eastern Old Testament context for New Testament understandings of the Trinity? | He says the topic is very specific and suggests targeted research rather than an easy single-source answer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=3913" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:13</a> | Who was the Assyrian high god? | He says the answer is not simple but identifies Asshur as central in Assyrian religion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=4095" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:15</a> | Is "Ur of the Chaldeans" in Genesis 15:7 a lexical gloss? | He checks the passage and treats the wording as a plausible explanatory place-name issue rather than a simple anachronism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=4311" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:51</a> | What is the importance of the donkey never having been ridden in Mark 11:2? | He says the unused animal has cultic implications because it has not been ritually defiled by ordinary use. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=4788" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:19:48</a> | Why does Luke say Heli was Joseph's father and not Mary's? | He explains the genealogy as functioning through Joseph legally while still addressing the maternal-line interpretation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=5037" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:57</a> | How much would it cost to fund commentaries for Exodus, Numbers, and Chronicles? | He says serious commentary projects are expensive because they require sustained scholarly time and support. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=5750" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:35:50</a> | What ancient law codes would Abraham have known or quoted? | He says very old law traditions are in play here, so parallels with early law collections are not a defeater for Abrahamic antiquity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=6350" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:50</a> | Was Israel's conquest of the promised land genocide, as some scholars suggest? | He says no, reading the conquest language as ancient hyperbolic warfare language rather than modern genocide language. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=7368" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:02:48</a> | How should the geography of Uz and the Sabeans in Job 1:13-17 be understood? | He treats the passage as geographically difficult but interpretable in ancient Near Eastern terms. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=8170" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:16:10</a> | Why does "The Rain in Spain" joke that Hebrews learn their language backwards? | He explains it as a joke about Hebrew being read right to left, not as a serious linguistic claim. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=9651" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:40:51</a> | In Jeremiah 13:23, why does the text mention the Kushite's skin and the leopard's spots? | He says it is a rhetorical question using things that do not change to make a moral point. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=9878" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:38</a> | Where can people find ancient Near Eastern maps they can edit and use publicly? | He says ready-made options are limited and leans toward building or commissioning maps instead of assuming easy copyright-free sources. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=10134" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:48:54</a> | How should someone respond to a pastor claiming to discern which songs are or are not inspired by the Holy Spirit? | He urges caution and grounds discernment in theology rather than a pastor's unsupported impressions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=10246" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:46</a> | What are his thoughts on Billy Carson? | He strongly criticizes Carson and treats his claims as conspiracy-driven rather than scholarly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=10343" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:23</a> | Is Isaiah 7:14 future prophecy, about Isaiah's own time, or both? | He says it can be both, with an immediate setting and a fuller future manifestation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=10769" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:59:29</a> | How should miracles noticed by foreign nations in 2 Kings 5:5 and 2 Chronicles 32:31 be understood? | He says foreign observers could notice Yahweh's acts, but the biblical point is not that surrounding nations fully understood them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=11640" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:14:00</a> | Can spiritual beings communicate telepathically or read minds? | He says they may communicate in ways analogous to telepathy, but they cannot read minds. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=12053" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:20:53</a> | What are his thoughts on Orthodox saints and miracle claims from Mount Athos or monasteries? | He is cautious about miracle claims and does not accept them uncritically. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=13320" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:42:00</a> | Are there any good Bible comics? | He says he does not know of good examples and is skeptical of the Bible manga he has seen. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/GRa11_37Vy8?t=13636" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:47:16</a> | What was the Tower of Babel built for, and how would it prevent people from being scattered? | He says the tower represents an urban centralizing project and a human attempt to resist dispersion. |
